philosophies: Read a lot, think clearly, act rarely.
Inversion: find what guarantees failure, then stop doing that.
Only act within your circle of competence. You don't need a view on everything.
Small, consistent actions compound. One good habit daily beats occasional intensity.
Think past the first consequence — ask: then what?
Sunk cost is gone. Decide only on what happens from here.
Understand the incentives — yours and theirs.
Build margin of safety. Things will go wrong, assume they will.
Never miss twice. One bad day doesn't become a bad week.
The goal is not to win arguments, but to avoid being wrong.
